result.summary: Zscaler exposes multiple authenticated OneAPI REST APIs plus a GraphQL Analytics API; I also found a Zscaler Integrations MCP server, but no public CLI integration surface was confirmed.

zscaler_oauth_client.label: Zscaler OneAPI OAuth client
zscaler_oauth_client.setup: In [Authentication Service](https://help.zscaler.com/authentication-service/what-authentication-service), create an API client for the product resource(s) you need and grant the required RBAC roles/scopes. Use the client credentials flow: store the client `id` and `secret` and exchange them for a bearer token. Zscaler recommends the client-secret path for lab/testing, with stronger JWT- or certificate-based methods for production.

zscaler_bearer_token.label: Zscaler OneAPI bearer token
zscaler_bearer_token.setup: Obtain via Zscaler OneAPI OAuth client credentials in [Authentication Service](https://help.zscaler.com/authentication-service/what-authentication-service). Exchange the client `id`/`secret` for an access token, then send it as `Authorization: Bearer ...`.
